description: Cross-language parity vectors for #2311 protocol contract hash. Python (claudemd-gen.py:_hash_fragment_set) and Rust (chorus-hooks protocol_contract) MUST produce identical digests on all fixtures. Any canonicalization change = regenerate from Python, update Rust, both sides must re-agree.
algorithm: sha256 over sorted entries; per entry: utf8(rel_path) || 0x00 || ascii(sha256_hex(content_bytes)) || 0x00

fixtures.name: empty
fixtures.description: Empty fragment set — canonical 'hash of nothing fed with the algorithm'. Both sides must agree that sha256() over no input yields the same digest.

fixtures.name: known_content
fixtures.description: Two synthetic fragments with fixed bytes. If canonicalization drifts (encoding, separator, sort order) this hash changes and both Python and Rust fail loud.

fixtures.name: live_core
fixtures.description: Current 13-fragment protocol core from the live repo. References paths — both Python gen and Rust hook read the real files. When any core fragment changes, this hash changes; vector must be regenerated and both sides update together.
